title: sambucus cerulea var. velutina
intro: Sambucus cerulea var. velutina, also known as the Velvet Elderberry, is a deciduous shrub native to the western United States and Canada. It is a variety of the Sambucus cerulea plant, which is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its berries, leaves, and flowers. The Velvet Elderberry has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the Velvet Elderberry, such as teas, tinctures, and supplements, can be used to support immune function, reduce fever, and promote overall health and well-being.
